meaning: n.枢轴,支点,(讨论的)中心点,重点adj.枢轴的vi.在枢轴上转动vt.装枢轴于
examples: Two years ago, CEO Chris Cocalis of Pivot Cycles—which sells high-end mountain bikes—found that bike shops weren't interested in stocking ebikes.
The wheels pivot for easy manoeuvring.
The pedal had sheared off at the pivot.
At the same time, I sought to discern the point around which my own transition would pivot.
At first glance, PeeWee PC's Pivot Tablet Laptop is a formidable rival to the long-standing OLPC XO.
